title: Align Capability Truth Matrix and documentation claims
outOfScope: Implementing calibration runtime, Quick Install digest verification, Bootstrap Wizard, Ownership Manifest, Update/Uninstall lifecycle, CI release evidence, or Installer decomposition
Changing release metadata, installer behavior, or product runtime code
Deleting historical Work Item, review, or release evidence

sources.reason: Current public capability and boundary claims to align with repository evidence.
Installation and calibration claims that must distinguish current runtime from planned behavior.
Installed lifecycle evidence and explicit deferred-stage boundaries.
Authoritative installer/runtime surface for the template repository.
Adopter-installed command surface and parity evidence.
Regression checks for matrix schema, status vocabulary, and documented current/planned claims.
Confirmed bounded Markdown budget reservation for the new evidence matrix and aligned documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: This Work Item changes the authoritative capability matrix and documentation claims only; later runtime implementation remains explicitly out of scope.

executionDecision.reason: The user explicitly authorized serial implementation of the full Conditional GO remediation plan, starting with the capability truth boundary.

preReviewWarnings: Reviewers must verify that every current claim links to a command, source, test, or release evidence, and that deferred runtime capabilities remain planned.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Create docs/reference/capability-truth-matrix.md with exactly the four status values defined by this Contract; every capability row names at least one command/source/test/release evidence path or an explicit missing-evidence reason.

Update README.md, README.zh-CN.md, README.ja.md, docs/getting-started/installation.md, docs/getting-started/installation.ja.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/reference/upgrade.md, and docs/reference/installed-lifecycle.md to link the matrix and label the ten-stage calibration, Candidate activation, Wizard, ownership manifest, and full lifecycle items as planned or evidence-bounded where current proof is absent.

Add tests/test_capability_truth_matrix.py with assertions for the four allowed statuses, required evidence fields, and explicit planned status for calibration, bootstrap, ownership, and release-evidence capabilities.
Run pytest -q tests/test_capability_truth_matrix.py and record the result as evidence for the matrix schema and deferred-status assertions.
Run make check-docs-metadata and make check-governance-complexity; both must pass with no documentation metadata or complexity-budget violation.
Run the Contract verification list through make ai-finish; no diff path may modify scripts/install_ai_cockpit.py, templates/make/Makefile.ai, release metadata, or runtime behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The Conditional GO review found that public README and design language leads the actual template/adopter capability in calibration, lifecycle, bootstrap, ownership, quick install, and release evidence. A single evidence-backed matrix is needed before implementing the later runtime Work Items.
constraints: Start from the latest origin/main after the completed plan-document Work Item.
Document current evidence conservatively and label unsupported capabilities planned; do not implement later Work Item scope.
Preserve the Repository Governance Layer boundary and enterprise-security NO-GO boundary.
Keep all historical Work Item and release evidence intact.
intent.rationale: A truth matrix makes documentation claims reviewable and gives later runtime Work Items a stable baseline; without it, implementation and release evidence can continue to mix template, installed, and planned states.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A documented current capability has a repository evidence reference.
scenarioCoverage.reason: This Work Item changes documentation and static regression checks; it does not execute an adopter runtime scenario.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Deferred calibration/runtime capabilities are not classified as implemented without evidence.
scenarioCoverage.reason: This Work Item changes documentation and static regression checks; runtime behavior belongs to later serial Work Items.

rawUserRequest: 开始实施上面的全部计划，严格按顺序完成每个工单、PR、合并、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步，完成以后发布新版本；不要跳过任何计划内工单。

declaredIntent.summary: Establish the evidence-backed Capability Truth Matrix and align public documentation before later runtime remediation Work Items.

closure.evidence: make ai-close-work-item TASK=conditional_go_review_execution_plan reported ready for next Work Item on synchronized main.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized implementation of the complete serial remediation plan, including documentation, tests, governance files, PRs, closure, and release.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
